title: carex fracta
intro: Carex fracta, also known as the Fragrant Sedge or Western Sedge, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Carex fracta include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial and fungal infections, and relieving congestion, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Carex fracta is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in a compress or poultice to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
